question: What four dimensions does the Time Traveller argue that all real bodies must have?
ground_truth_answer: Length, Breadth, Thickness, and Duration (Time)
context_hint: any real body must have extension in _four_ directions: it must have Length, Breadth, Thickness, and—Duration

question: What is the Fourth Dimension according to the Time Traveller?
ground_truth_answer: Time is the Fourth Dimension; it is only another way of looking at Time
context_hint: Really this is what is meant by the Fourth Dimension, though some people who talk about the Fourth Dimension do not know they mean it. It is only another way of looking at Time.

question: What was the name of the Eloi girl who became the Time Traveller's companion?
ground_truth_answer: Weena
context_hint: found that her name was Weena

question: What did the Time Traveller find inside the pedestal of the White Sphinx?
ground_truth_answer: The Time Machine
context_hint: Time Machine was inside that pedestal

question: What were the two species that humanity had evolved into by the far future?
ground_truth_answer: The Eloi and the Morlocks
context_hint: the name by which these creatures were called

question: What did the people of the year Eight Hundred and Two Thousand ask the Time Traveller?
ground_truth_answer: They asked if he had come from the sun in a thunderstorm
context_hint: asked me, in fact, if I had come from the sun in a thunderstorm!

question: What relationship did the Eloi and Morlocks have in the far future?
ground_truth_answer: The Eloi had decayed to beautiful futility while the Morlocks maintained them and their garments through an old habit of service
context_hint: The Eloi, like the Carlovignan kings, had decayed to a mere beautiful futility.

question: What did the Time Traveller say about our consciousness and the Time dimension?
ground_truth_answer: Our consciousness moves along the Time-Dimension with a uniform velocity from the cradle to the grave
context_hint: Our mental existences, which are immaterial and have no dimensions, are passing along the Time-Dimension with a uniform velocity from the cradle to the grave.

question: Who first sent the model Time Machine on its career?
ground_truth_answer: The Psychologist
context_hint: Psychologist himself who sent forth the model Time Machine on its career

question: What was the Time Traveller's emotional reaction when he noticed the Eloi's intellectual level?
ground_truth_answer: A flow of disappointment rushed across his mind and he felt he had built the Time Machine in vain
context_hint: A flow of disappointment rushed across my mind. For a moment I felt that I had built the Time Machine in vain.

question: What type of food did the Eloi primarily eat?
ground_truth_answer: Fruits
context_hint: upon these were heaps of fruits.

question: What did the Time Traveller say about his chairs?
ground_truth_answer: The chairs embraced and caressed their occupants rather than submitted to be sat upon
context_hint: Our chairs, being his patents, embraced and caressed us rather than submitted to be sat upon

question: How did the Medical Man challenge the Time Traveller's argument about vertical movement?
ground_truth_answer: He pointed out that there are balloons for vertical movement
context_hint: Not exactly," said the Medical Man. "There are balloons.

question: What diagram did the Time Traveller use to illustrate Time as a dimension?
ground_truth_answer: A weather record showing the barometer's movement as a line traced along the Time-Dimension
context_hint: Here is a popular scientific diagram, a weather record. This line I trace with my finger shows the movement of the barometer.

question: Who was Professor Simon Newcomb and what had he been expounding?
ground_truth_answer: Professor Simon Newcomb was expounding Four-Dimensional geometry to the New York Mathematical Society
context_hint: Professor Simon Newcomb was expounding this to the New York Mathematical Society only a month or so ago.

question: What was Filby's argument against time travel?
ground_truth_answer: He said it was against reason
context_hint: It's against reason," said Filby.

question: Why did the Eloi have a terrible fear of the dark?
ground_truth_answer: Because the Morlocks came out at night in darkness to prey upon them
context_hint: why were they so terribly afraid of the dark?

question: What did the Time Traveller use to startle the people of the far future?
ground_truth_answer: He gave them a vivid rendering of a thunderclap
context_hint: I nodded, pointed to the sun, and gave them such a vivid rendering of a thunderclap as startled them.

question: What material were the Eloi's robes made of?
ground_truth_answer: Soft yet strong, silky material
context_hint: clad in the same soft, and yet strong, silky material.

question: What did the Morlocks do for the Eloi in the far future?
ground_truth_answer: The Morlocks made the Eloi's garments and maintained them in their habitual needs through the survival of an old habit of service
context_hint: And the Morlocks made their garments, I inferred, and maintained them in their habitual needs, perhaps through the survival of an old habit of service.

question: What flowers did the Eloi bring to the Time Traveller?
ground_truth_answer: A chain of beautiful flowers altogether new to him
context_hint: carrying a chain of beautiful flowers altogether new to me, and put it about my neck.

question: What natural infirmity does the Time Traveller blame for humans not perceiving Time as a fourth dimension?
ground_truth_answer: A natural infirmity of the flesh causes us to overlook Time as a dimension
context_hint: But through a natural infirmity of the flesh, which I will explain to you in a moment, we incline to overlook this fact.

question: What was the Time Traveller's theory about who had taken his Time Machine?
ground_truth_answer: He felt sure the Morlocks had taken it
context_hint: Why had the Morlocks taken my Time Machine? For I felt sure it was they who had taken it.

question: What do the four dimensions of space correspond to according to the Time Traveller?
ground_truth_answer: Three dimensions of Space (Length, Breadth, Thickness) and Time as the fourth dimension
context_hint: There are really four dimensions, three which we call the three planes of Space, and a fourth, Time.

question: How did the Eloi receive the Time Traveller's flowers around his neck?
ground_truth_answer: The idea was received with melodious applause and they all started running to bring more flowers
context_hint: The idea was received with melodious applause; and presently they were all running to and fro for flowers

question: What did the Time Traveller mean by saying we can go up against gravitation?
ground_truth_answer: Humans can go up in a balloon and similarly might learn to stop or accelerate movement along the Time-Dimension
context_hint: He can go up against gravitation in a balloon, and why should he not hope that ultimately he may be able to stop or accelerate his drift along the Time-Dimension

question: How did the Eloi greet the Time Traveller when he first appeared?
ground_truth_answer: They all withdrew a pace and bowed
context_hint: They all withdrew a pace or so and bowed.

question: How did the Eloi address the Time Traveller before he showed them the model machine?
ground_truth_answer: He was expounding a recondite matter to his guests who sat and lazily admired his earnestness
context_hint: we sat and lazily admired his earnestness over this new paradox

question: What did the Time Traveller think about the relationship between Time and Space?
ground_truth_answer: There is no difference between Time and any of the three dimensions of Space except that our consciousness moves along it
context_hint: There is no difference between Time and any of the three dimensions of Space except that our consciousness moves along it.

question: How did the Time Traveller first try to communicate with the Eloi?
ground_truth_answer: He pointed to the Time Machine and to himself
context_hint: I pointed to the Time Machine and to myself.

question: What did Alice see the White Rabbit take out of its waistcoat-pocket?
ground_truth_answer: A watch
context_hint: the Rabbit actually _took a watch out of its waistcoat-pocket_, and looked at it

question: What label was on the bottle Alice found on the glass table?
ground_truth_answer: DRINK ME
context_hint: round the neck of the bottle was a paper label, with the words "DRINK ME," beautifully printed on it in large letters

question: What did Alice find written on the little cake she found in the glass box?
ground_truth_answer: EAT ME
context_hint: found in it a very small cake, on which the words "EAT ME" were beautifully marked in currants

question: What was the name of Alice's cat?
ground_truth_answer: Dinah
context_hint: Dinah was the cat

question: What did the Dodo declare the winner of the Caucus-race?
ground_truth_answer: Everybody has won, and all must have prizes
context_hint: _Everybody_ has won, and all must have prizes

question: What advice did the Caterpillar give Alice about the mushroom?
ground_truth_answer: One side will make you grow taller, and the other side will make you grow shorter
context_hint: One side will make you grow taller, and the other side will make you grow shorter

question: Why did the Duchess's cat grin?
ground_truth_answer: It was a Cheshire cat, and that is why
context_hint: It's a Cheshire cat, and that's why. Pig!

question: What did the Cheshire Cat say when Alice said she did not care where she went?
ground_truth_answer: Then it doesn't matter which way you go
context_hint: Then it doesn't matter which way you go

question: What were the mallets, balls, and arches made of in the Queen's croquet game?
ground_truth_answer: The mallets were live flamingoes, the balls were live hedgehogs, and the arches were doubled-up soldiers
context_hint: the balls were live hedgehogs, the mallets live flamingoes, and the soldiers had to double themselves up and to stand on their hands and feet, to make the arches

question: What was the Queen's constant order during the croquet game?
ground_truth_answer: Off with his head! or Off with her head!
context_hint: shouting "Off with his head!" or "Off with her head!" about once in a minute

question: What were the gardeners painting when Alice first entered the Queen's garden?
ground_truth_answer: They were painting white roses red
context_hint: the roses growing on it were white, but there were three gardeners at it, busily painting them red

question: Why were the gardeners painting the roses red?
ground_truth_answer: They had planted a white rose tree by mistake instead of a red one, and feared the Queen would have their heads cut off if she found out
context_hint: this here ought to have been a _red_ rose-tree, and we put a white one in by mistake; and if the Queen was to find it out, we should all have our heads cut off

question: What riddle did the Hatter ask Alice at the Mad Tea-Party?
ground_truth_answer: Why is a raven like a writing-desk?
context_hint: Why is a raven like a writing-desk?

question: Why was it always tea-time at the Mad Tea-Party?
ground_truth_answer: The Hatter had quarrelled with Time and Time stopped at six o'clock, so they could never wash the things and it was always tea-time
context_hint: it's always tea-time, and we've no time to wash the things between whiles

question: What did the Dormouse say that the three sisters at the bottom of the well lived on?
ground_truth_answer: Treacle
context_hint: They lived on treacle

question: How small did Alice become after drinking from the bottle labelled DRINK ME?
ground_truth_answer: She was now only ten inches high
context_hint: she was now only ten inches high, and her face brightened up at the thought that she was now the right size for going through the little door

question: What did the Pigeon call Alice?
ground_truth_answer: The Pigeon called Alice a Serpent because of her long neck
context_hint: Serpent, I say again!

question: What was Alice's first thought about the tiny golden key she found?
ground_truth_answer: It might belong to one of the doors of the hall
context_hint: a tiny golden key, and Alice's first thought was that it might belong to one of the doors of the hall

question: What did the Cheshire Cat say about everyone in Wonderland?
ground_truth_answer: We're all mad here
context_hint: we're all mad here. I'm mad. You're mad.

question: How did the Cheshire Cat vanish the final time Alice watched it?
ground_truth_answer: It vanished quite slowly, beginning with the end of the tail, and ending with the grin, which remained some time after the rest of it had gone
context_hint: it vanished quite slowly, beginning with the end of the tail, and ending with the grin, which remained some time after the rest of it had gone

question: What was the opening invocation of The Odyssey?
ground_truth_answer: Tell me, O Muse, of that ingenious hero who travelled far and wide after he had sacked the famous town of Troy
context_hint: Tell me, O Muse, of that ingenious hero who travelled far and wide after he had sacked the famous town of Troy

question: Which god persecuted Ulysses and prevented him from getting home?
ground_truth_answer: Neptune (Poseidon)
context_hint: Neptune, who still persecuted him without ceasing and would not let him get home

question: Why did Neptune hate Ulysses?
ground_truth_answer: Because Ulysses had blinded an eye of Polyphemus king of the Cyclopes, who was son to Neptune
context_hint: Neptune is still furious with Ulysses for having blinded an eye of Polyphemus king of the Cyclopes. Polyphemus is son to Neptune

question: Who was detaining Ulysses on an island at the start of The Odyssey?
ground_truth_answer: The goddess Calypso, who wanted to marry him
context_hint: was detained by the goddess Calypso, who had got him into a large cave and wanted to marry him

question: In what disguise did Minerva visit Telemachus at the start of The Odyssey?
ground_truth_answer: She disguised herself as Mentes, chief of the Taphians
context_hint: she was in Ithaca, at the gateway of Ulysses' house, disguised as a visitor, Mentes, chief of the Taphians

question: What trick did Penelope use to delay the suitors for three years?
ground_truth_answer: She worked on a great pall for Laertes all day, then unpicked the stitches by torchlight at night
context_hint: working on her great web all day long, but at night she would unpick the stitches again by torchlight. She fooled us in this way for three years

question: What false name did Ulysses give the Cyclops when asked?
ground_truth_answer: Noman (Nobody)
context_hint: my name is Noman; this is what my father and mother and my friends have always called me

question: How did Ulysses blind the Cyclops Polyphemus?
ground_truth_answer: He drove a sharpened olive-wood stake heated in the fire into the monster's eye
context_hint: We drove the sharp end of the beam into the monster's eye, and bearing upon it with all my weight I kept turning it round and round

question: How did Ulysses and his men escape from the Cyclops's cave?
ground_truth_answer: They hid under the bellies of the rams as they left the cave in the morning
context_hint: bound them noiselessly in threes together, with some of the withies on which the wicked monster used to sleep. There was to be a man under the middle sheep

question: What did Circe instruct Ulysses to do to protect his men from the Sirens?
ground_truth_answer: Stop his men's ears with wax and have himself lashed to the mast if he wished to listen
context_hint: stop your men's ears with wax that none of them may hear; but if you like you can listen yourself, for you may get the men to bind you as you stand upright on a cross piece half way up the mast

question: What happened when Ulysses wanted to be freed from the mast while hearing the Sirens?
ground_truth_answer: His men bound him with still stronger bonds and quickened their stroke until they were out of earshot
context_hint: I made signs by frowning to my men that they should set me free; but they quickened their stroke, and Eurylochus and Perimedes bound me with still stronger bonds till we had got out of hearing of the Sirens' voices

question: What did Calypso promise to give Ulysses when she agreed to let him leave?
ground_truth_answer: She promised to put bread, wine, and water on board, give him clothes, and send a fair wind to take him home
context_hint: I will put bread, wine, and water on board to save you from starving. I will also give you clothes, and will send you a fair wind to take you home

question: Where did Telemachus travel to seek news of his father?
ground_truth_answer: He went to Pylos to see Nestor and to Sparta to see Menelaus
context_hint: I am going to Sparta and to Pylos to see if I can hear anything about the return of my dear father

question: What did Menelaus say about Ulysses that moved Telemachus to weep?
ground_truth_answer: That no one of all the Achaeans worked so hard or risked so much as Ulysses did
context_hint: no one of all the Achaeans worked so hard or risked so much as he did. He took nothing by it, and has left a legacy of sorrow to myself

question: How did Minerva encourage Telemachus to set off on his voyage?
ground_truth_answer: She told him if he were made of the same stuff as his father he would be neither fool nor coward henceforward
context_hint: if you are made of the same stuff as your father you will be neither fool nor coward henceforward, for Ulysses never broke his word nor left his work half done

question: What were the suitors doing in Ulysses' house throughout the story?
ground_truth_answer: They were eating up his estate, sacrificing his oxen and sheep for their banquets, and courting Penelope against her will
context_hint: day by day they keep hanging about my father's house, sacrificing our oxen, sheep, and fat goats for their banquets, and never giving so much as a thought to the quantity of wine they drink

question: What omen appeared when Telemachus addressed the assembly about the suitors?
ground_truth_answer: Jove sent two eagles from the top of the mountain which flew over the assembly
context_hint: Jove sent two eagles from the top of the mountain, and they flew on and on with the wind, sailing side by side in their own lordly flight

question: What did the prophet Halitherses say the omen of the eagles meant?
ground_truth_answer: He said Ulysses was not going to be away much longer and was close at hand to deal out death and destruction on the suitors
context_hint: Ulysses is not going to be away much longer; indeed he is close at hand to deal out death and destruction, not on them alone, but on many another of us who live in Ithaca

question: What did Menelaus say he would have done for Ulysses had he returned safely from Troy?
ground_truth_answer: He said he would have founded a city for him in Argos, built him a house, and made him leave Ithaca with all his people
context_hint: I should have founded a city for him in Argos, and built him a house. I should have made him leave Ithaca with his goods, his son, and all his people

question: What did the nurse Euryclea do when Telemachus asked her to provision his voyage?
ground_truth_answer: She began to cry and begged him not to go, saying his father was dead in some foreign country and these wicked ones would scheme against him
context_hint: My dear child, what ever can have put such notion as that into your head? Where in the world do you want to go to
